# Abstract Bundle — Chapter 5 (BAD: fabricated audit metadata)

## Abstract

This chapter investigates how outcome-based accreditation frameworks shape
faculty workload distribution across research-intensive universities. Using
mixed methods across six case institutions, the study finds that accreditation
cycles introduce measurable workload reallocation with heterogeneous effects
by institutional tier. Implications for accreditation policy design are discussed.
